title: Tectaria christovalensis
taxonomy: Tectaria christovalensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Tectaria, which consists of approximately 180 species of ferns. The classification of Tectaria christovalensis also places it in the order Polypodiales, a large group of ferns that are characterized by the presence of a sori (spore-bearing organs) on the underside of the fronds. The family Dryopteridaceae is a family of ferns that are commonly known as the wood ferns. Tectaria christovalensis is a relatively rare species found in the wild, particularly in regions with high levels of humidity and moisture.
